title: Esports DB
description: PandaScore esports data — matches, teams, players, tournaments, leagues.

game_overview.title: Game Coverage Overview
game_overview.description: How many leagues, series and tournaments does each game have? A quick snapshot of coverage across all titles.
game_overview.sql: SELECT v.name AS game, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM leagues WHERE videogame_id = v.id) AS leagues, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM series WHERE videogame_id = v.id) AS series, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tournaments WHERE videogame_id = v.id) AS tournaments FROM videogames v ORDER BY leagues DESC

leagues_by_game.title: Leagues for a Game
leagues_by_game.description: Browse every league for a specific title. Try: Counter-Strike, Valorant, Dota 2, LoL, Overwatch, Rocket League, PUBG.
leagues_by_game.sql: SELECT v.name AS game, l.name AS league, l.url, COUNT(DISTINCT s.id) AS series_count FROM leagues l JOIN videogames v ON v.id = l.videogame_id LEFT JOIN series s ON s.league_id = l.id WHERE v.name LIKE '%' || :game || '%' GROUP BY l.id ORDER BY series_count DESC

top_leagues_by_activity.title: Most Active Leagues
top_leagues_by_activity.description: Which leagues have the most series? A ranking of the busiest competitive circuits across all games.
top_leagues_by_activity.sql: SELECT l.name AS league, v.name AS game, COUNT(s.id) AS series_count FROM leagues l JOIN videogames v ON v.id = l.videogame_id JOIN series s ON s.league_id = l.id GROUP BY l.id ORDER BY series_count DESC LIMIT 50

premier_tournaments.title: Premier (S-Tier) Tournaments
premier_tournaments.description: The most prestigious events in esports — S-tier tournaments only. These are the Majors, Worlds, and Grand Finals. Prize pool is shown as amount + currency where available.
premier_tournaments.sql: SELECT t.name, v.name AS game, l.name AS league, t.begin_at, t.end_at, t.prizepool FROM tournaments t JOIN videogames v ON v.id = t.videogame_id JOIN leagues l ON l.id = t.league_id WHERE t.tier = 's' ORDER BY t.begin_at DESC LIMIT 100

tournaments_with_prize_pools.title: Tournaments with Prize Pools
tournaments_with_prize_pools.description: Events that had prize money on the line. Prize pool is shown as amount + currency (e.g. '70000 United States Dollar'). Shows game and league context.
tournaments_with_prize_pools.sql: SELECT t.name, v.name AS game, l.name AS league, t.prizepool, t.tier, t.begin_at, t.end_at FROM tournaments t JOIN videogames v ON v.id = t.videogame_id JOIN leagues l ON l.id = t.league_id WHERE t.prizepool IS NOT NULL AND t.prizepool != '' ORDER BY t.begin_at DESC LIMIT 100

series_by_year.title: Competitive Seasons by Year
series_by_year.description: All series (competitive seasons) for a given year. Shows which leagues were active and in which games, plus how many tournaments ran under each series.
series_by_year.sql: SELECT s.full_name AS series, v.name AS game, l.name AS league, s.season, s.begin_at, s.end_at, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tournaments WHERE serie_id = s.id) AS tournament_count FROM series s JOIN videogames v ON v.id = s.videogame_id JOIN leagues l ON l.id = s.league_id WHERE s.year = cast(:year as integer) ORDER BY v.name, l.name, s.begin_at

league_history.title: League Season History
league_history.description: All series and tournaments ever run under a specific league. Enter a league name (or part of it) to trace its full history.
league_history.sql: SELECT l.name AS league, s.year, s.full_name AS series, s.season, s.begin_at, s.end_at, COUNT(t.id) AS tournaments FROM series s JOIN leagues l ON l.id = s.league_id LEFT JOIN tournaments t ON t.serie_id = s.id WHERE l.name LIKE '%' || :league || '%' GROUP BY s.id ORDER BY s.year DESC, s.begin_at DESC

tournament_tier_breakdown.title: Tournament Tiers by Game
tournament_tier_breakdown.description: How many S/A/B/C/D-tier events exist per game? Tiers reflect prestige: S = premier, A = top, B = mid, C/D = regional/open. Events with no tier assigned are shown as '(none)'.
tournament_tier_breakdown.sql: SELECT v.name AS game, COALESCE(NULLIF(t.tier, ''), '(none)') AS tier, COUNT(*) AS tournament_count FROM tournaments t JOIN videogames v ON v.id = t.videogame_id GROUP BY v.id, tier ORDER BY v.name, CASE tier WHEN 's' THEN 1 WHEN 'a' THEN 2 WHEN 'b' THEN 3 WHEN 'c' THEN 4 WHEN 'd' THEN 5 WHEN 'unranked' THEN 6 ELSE 7 END

search_leagues.title: Search Leagues
search_leagues.description: Find a league by name — useful for looking up circuits like ESL, IEM, VCT, DreamLeague, PGL, BLAST, etc.
search_leagues.sql: SELECT l.name AS league, v.name AS game, l.url, COUNT(DISTINCT s.id) AS series_count FROM leagues l JOIN videogames v ON v.id = l.videogame_id LEFT JOIN series s ON s.league_id = l.id WHERE l.name LIKE '%' || :search || '%' GROUP BY l.id ORDER BY series_count DESC

recent_tournaments.title: Most Recent Tournaments
recent_tournaments.description: The latest completed tournaments across all games, sorted by end date.
recent_tournaments.sql: SELECT t.name, v.name AS game, l.name AS league, t.tier, t.prizepool, t.begin_at, t.end_at FROM tournaments t JOIN videogames v ON v.id = t.videogame_id JOIN leagues l ON l.id = t.league_id WHERE t.end_at IS NOT NULL ORDER BY t.end_at DESC LIMIT 50

upcoming_tournaments.title: Upcoming Tournaments
upcoming_tournaments.description: Tournaments not yet started, sorted by earliest start date first. Covers all games and tiers.
upcoming_tournaments.sql: SELECT t.name, v.name AS game, l.name AS league, t.tier, t.prizepool, t.begin_at, t.end_at FROM tournaments t JOIN videogames v ON v.id = t.videogame_id JOIN leagues l ON l.id = t.league_id WHERE t.begin_at > date('now') ORDER BY t.begin_at ASC LIMIT 100

coverage_by_year.title: Historical Coverage by Year
coverage_by_year.description: How many tournaments were recorded per game per year? Use this to understand how far back the data goes and which titles have the deepest history.
coverage_by_year.sql: SELECT strftime('%Y', t.begin_at) AS year, v.name AS game, COUNT(*) AS tournaments FROM tournaments t JOIN videogames v ON v.id = t.videogame_id WHERE t.begin_at IS NOT NULL GROUP BY year, v.id ORDER BY year DESC, tournaments DESC
